sourceNote: AFM-only training transcription from Viking Air PSM 1-63-1A Revision 53, Section 3.3.3, pages 3-12 to 3-14. The memory list follows the four numbered immediate actions in the AFM; the conditional partial/total-power and manual-feather logic remains nested within action 4. Secondary actions begin only after reaching a safe altitude, typically several thousand feet AGL. Crew-role fields are trainer UI assignments only; they are not approved PF/PM callouts or operator SOP. Verify applicability against the aircraft-specific AFM, amendments, supplements, and operator procedures.

memory.action: Power levers — Set Maximum Power. Advance both power levers to the torque, T5, or NP limit, whichever is reached first; ensure both PROP levers are at MAX RPM (96% NP).
FLAP position indicator — Confirm flaps are set to 10°.
Aircraft control — Adjust pitch attitude to maintain 80 KIAS; maintain directional control with rudder.

Propeller of inoperative engine — Confirm that the propeller has automatically feathered. If it has not, determine whether the power loss is partial or total. If the loss is partial, it may be appropriate to leave the affected engine operating if it is contributing thrust. If the loss is total, manually FEATHER the propeller of the inoperative engine.

flow.action: Climb to a safe altitude, typically several thousand feet AGL. If turns are required for obstacle clearance, limit bank angle to 15°.
Power lever of inoperative engine — Retard to the 10 PSI torque position, approximately zero thrust.
PROP lever of inoperative engine — FEATHER.
FUEL lever of inoperative engine — OFF.
BOOST PUMP switch for inoperative engine — OFF.
GENERATOR switch of inoperative engine — OFF.
BLEED AIR switch of inoperative engine — OFF.
FUEL OFF emergency shut-off switch of inoperative engine — OFF.
PROP AUTOFEATHER switch — OFF.
Check generator load on the operative engine; reduce electrical consumption as required to remain at or below 1.0 on the loadmeter.
Use fuel as necessary to remain within center-of-gravity limits for the remainder of the flight.
Complete the AFTER TAKE-OFF checklist, Section 4.11.
